computation-expressions.comment: The three built-in CE builders, only when opening a block.
computation-expressions.name: keyword.other.computation.pyfun

patterns.comment: `let mut name` — `mut` is the mutability escape hatch (opt-out of immutable-by-default). Honest scope (it *is* a storage modifier) + a `.mutable` tail the extension pins to an amber 'caution' colour via configurationDefaults.
`let [pure] name` / `let! name` — colour the bound name as a function/binding. `pure` is an encouraged effect assertion, so it stays a neutral storage modifier.
`extern` — the untyped-on-the-Python-side FFI boundary, effectful-by-default; an escape hatch. Honest keyword scope + a `.extern` tail the extension pins to the same amber 'caution' colour.
patterns.name: keyword.other.extern.pyfun

patterns.comment: Control-flow keywords. `case` is a match arm (DESIGN.md §7.2); `match e:` / `case pat [if guard]: body`. `with` now only introduces a record update `{ p with x = 3 }`.
patterns.name: keyword.control.pyfun

numbers.comment: Integer/float literals, with an optional adjacent unit-of-measure annotation (e.g. 5<m>, 3.0<m/s^2>). The unit must touch the digits — matching Pyfun's lexer rule that distinguishes 5<m> from 5 < m.

patterns.comment: Built-in collection modules used as a namespace (before a dot).
patterns.name: entity.name.namespace.pyfun

patterns.comment: Type names and data constructors: any upper-case-led identifier.
patterns.name: entity.name.type.pyfun

patterns.comment: `<-` reassignment — the act of mutation. Honest operator scope + a `.mutation` tail pinned to the same amber 'caution' colour as `mut`.
patterns.name: keyword.operator.mutation.pyfun
